results.text:
An Honest Ritual Multivitamins Review: Everything to Know | 2023

Sharing an in-depth Ritual multivitamins review today! I mentioned Ritual as part of my morning routine in my recent podcast episode, and I wanted to get into all the details about Ritual and why I’m such a fan.

You’ve likely seen it all over the ‘gram, but what’s the hype really about? I’ve been taking Ritual multivitamins daily for several months now, so I’m ready to dive into my Ritual multivitamins review!

Psst! Be sure to use code WTFAB3 for 10% off your first 3 months!

Sign up for Ritual today! >>

## Ritual Multivitamins Review

### Traceability and transparency.

First and foremost, I appreciate that Ritual is intentionally open and transparent about its sources, studies, and suppliers. Their multivitamin is the first of its kind to be made with a visible supply chain. You can learn about their sourcing and transparency efforts here.

### Their ingredients are on point.

Ritual multivitamins for women are formulated with key ingredients to help fill gaps in nutrients in women’s diets. They’re vegan-friendly (they use algal oil instead of fish oil for omega 3s), non-GMO, gluten and major-allergen-free. They are formulated without shady additives or artificial colorants, which as I was doing research on what I wanted my multivitamin to be I realized is rare! We don’t necessarily think about it and it’s easy to assume that if it’s on a shelf at a store it’s ok to ingest, but when you actually look at what is in these supplements, it’s not always pretty. And it’s so important to know for your health because you’re putting this into your body. Ritual offers me that peace of mind since they are so transparent about their ingredients.*

### Foundational health.

Ritual’s multivitamins are designed with supporting foundational health in mind. From Omega3’s and vitamin B12 to support brain health to calcium-helper nutrients Vitamin D, Magnesium and Boron to help maintain bone health.*

### Ritual is female-founded.

When she became pregnant, CEO and founder Katerina Schneider was purging household products and swapping them for products with better ingredients. But during her quest for a prenatal multivitamin, all the ones she came across she couldn’t get behind. Since she couldn’t find a brand that she trusted, she decided to build her own.

### They’re sustainable.

Each Ritual bottle is made with 100% recycled materials, and their mailers are made from recycled newsprint and plant fibers. They also work to offset carbon emissions from each Ritual shipment.

### They have multivitamins for different life stages.

I personally take Ritual’s Essential for Women 18+ multivitamin. You can check out all of the Ritual Essential multivitamins here, but they cover essential prenatal multivitamins to postnatal, different age groups for men and women, and teens and kids.

### They’re great for sensitive stomachs.

Ritual’s capsules have a delayed-release design. I can take them on an emp

results.text:
Ritual Vitamins Review: The Best Clean Multi-Vitamin Brand I Can't Stop Raving About

# Ritual Vitamins Review: The Best Clean Multi-Vitamin Brand I Can’t Stop Raving About

With so many supplement brands out there, it’s hard to figure out which is best for you. When I was searching for a multi-vitamin, I wanted to ensure the pills I was consuming daily were doing more good than harm to my body. I didn’t want any sugary ingredients or any artificial colorants. Better health starts with better ingredients, and the brand Ritual promises just that. Everyone was raving about it on social media and now I understand why. I’ve been taking Ritual for a year now, and my Ritual Vitamins review highlights how they differ from other vitamins I’ve taken in the past.

If you’re just here for the promo code: use ELAINE for 10% off your first 3 months of Ritual

## My Ritual Vitamins Review

### They Taste Minty Fresh

I was so surprised that these vitamins have a minty fresh taste! Other vitamins I’ve tried in the past literally almost made me gag because they tasted so bad. If a vitamin tastes like crap, you’ll probably not keep taking it every day.

### They’re Perfect for Sensitive Stomachs

Ritual vitamins are the only ones I can take on an empty stomach without feeling nauseous*. They designed the capsules to be delayed-released which means they dissolve later in the small intestine, which is an ideal place to absorb nutrients. I would recommend Ritual to anyone who has a sensitive stomach.

### Two Pills a Day is all You Need

I’m not a fan of taking more than two pills a day. Other vitamins I’ve tried recommended four pills daily! That’s insane. Ritual’s vitamins are small, but they can pack all their essential nutrients into a tiny capsule! Oh, and they’re so damn pretty looking too!

### You Never Have to remember when to re-order your Vitamins

When you sign up for their subscription, you never have to worry about re-ordering a new bottle when you start running low.

### You get what you pay for

Ritual’s monthly subscription is $30 for 60 capsules (a 30-day supply). There are other less expensive brands, but if you’re looking for quality ingredients and transparency, then Ritual is the brand for you.

### Full Transparency on the Ingredients

Like I said before if I’m taking these capsules daily, I need to know what is going into my body. Ritual’s vitamins are non-dairy, non-GMO, gluten-free, and vegan. If you go to the Ritual website, you can see sourcing and manufacturing information for every single ingredient.

Most importantly to me, Ritual multi-vitamins 18+ are USP verified. This means that the United States Pharmacopeial Convention rigorously tested the product for ingredient identity, potency, purity, and performance.

Here is a list of what’s in the Ritual Vitamin capsules:

- Vitamin K2 MK7 – Helps to support bone health*
- Vitamin E – Helps to fight free radicals*
- Vitamin D3 – Supports your bone health*
- Vitamin B12 – Helps support ene

# The Prenatal Multivitamin We Recommend For Expecting Moms

Words by

##### Jenni Stein

Share

Published on:

Nov 10, 2022

In partnership with Ritual. Editor’s Note: We only select and work with partners that meet our quality standards, so you can rest assured we only endorse products we believe in.

Trying for a baby is hard. Being pregnant is hard. Preparing for a new baby is hard. I get it…and I’m here to tell you it doesn’t get easier. Just kidding, kind of. But I digress – we’re here to talk about multivitamins!

When my husband and I began talking about starting a family, I knew I wanted to find a great prenatal multivitamin to support my body during pregnancy. When I started researching, I quickly discovered that the market is not only super saturated, but it’s really hard to figure out which key nutrients are important for our bodies, and which brands actually sell a clean multivitamin.

Lucky for me, Ritual released its Essential for Women Prenatal MultiVitamin($39) and just like that, my decision was made.

These multivitamins were obsessively developed by scientists and nutritionists (credit: Ritual)

#### The Prenatal That Packs A Punch

With 12 key nutrients, this prenatal multivitamin has what you want, and perhaps more importantly, nothing you don’t. The star? Methylated folate (as 5-MTHF), the active form of folic acid – an important nutrient to help support neural tube development. This ingredient was particularly important to me, and many prenatal vitamins don’t include it. You can read more about methylated folate here.*

Rounding out an all-star ingredient list are choline, also for neural tube development support; vegan Omega-3 DHA, choline and folate to support fetal brain development; iron, folate and methylated B-12 to help support red blood cell formation; and calcium-helper nutrients D3, K2, magnesium and boron to help maintain bone health. All pretty important, don’t you think?*

#### When It Comes To Transparency, More Is More

As a brand, Ritual’s big thing is transparency, and it’s one reason I love the company so much. Each ingredient is traceable back to its source material, so you really feel like you understand exactly what’s going into your body. Plus, the brand’s website contains critical information, like the form, source material and function of every labeled ingredient.

Take Omega-3 DHA for example. Here, you’ll discover exactly why it’s important and its final place of manufacture (down to an in-depth supplier profile).* With thousands of vitamins lining the aisles of your local drugstore, it’s hard not to wonder: where did these ingredients come from? Are they the best possible version available? Sourced responsibly?

And let’s not forget – what’s not in your vitamin is just as important as what is. Ritual’s 12 key nutrients are meant to help fill nutrient gaps in the diet, not overload the bo

results.title: Ritual Vitamins Review 2026: I Tested Them for 90 Days!
results.author: Heather
results.text:
Ritual Vitamins Review 2026: I Tested Them for 90 Days! 

 

 

 

 

 

Table of Contents

Toggle

### ⚡ Quick Take: Ritual Vitamins 2026

Bottom Line: High-quality, transparent multivitamins with science-backed ingredients and traceable sourcing. Best for those who value clean formulations over mega-dosing.

Price: $39/month (up from $33) | Rating: ★★★★☆ 4.3/5

Best For: Health-conscious consumers seeking transparent, research-backed vitamins without questionable fillers

Skip If: You want a comprehensive multivitamin with 30+ ingredients or prefer one-time purchases over subscriptions

Better Living has partnered with Ritual, but only endorses products we love and believe in.

Ritual is a subscription wellness company offering clean, transparent, research-backed multivitamins and supplements. But with prices increasing and more competitors entering the “clean vitamin” space, are they still worth it in 2026?

I’ve been testing Ritual’s Essential for Women 18+ for 90 days. Here’s my honest take on quality, effectiveness, value, and whether the hype matches reality.

🔥 Get 40% Off Your First Month →

60-day money-back guarantee • Cancel anytime

---

## Why the Supplement Industry Has a Transparency Problem

Before diving into Ritual specifically, you need to understand why this company exists in the first place.

Here’s the uncomfortable truth: what’s on your supplement label doesn’t always match what’s inside. The FDA classifies supplements as food, not pharmaceuticals, which means regulation is far less stringent.

#### ⚠️ The Research Is Alarming

GAO Study: 2 out of 3 memory supplements tested contained NO active ingredient, and one contained unknown chemicals.

JAMA Network Open (2023) tested 57 sports supplements:

- The Bottom Line: Should You Try Ritual in 2026?
- 89% had incorrect quantities of listed ingredients
- 40% had undetectable amounts of ingredients on labels
- 12% contained FDA-banned ingredients

Sources: GAO Report, JAMA Study

Common problematic additives found in many multivitamins:

- Titanium dioxide – FDA-approved filler linked to allergies, autoimmune disorders, organ toxicity, and certain cancers (4, 5, 6, 7)
- Artificial colors and flavors – Often petroleum-derived (8, 9, 10, 11)
- Unnecessary binders and fillers – Added for texture and bulk, not health benefits

This is the environment Ritual was created to disrupt. But do they actually deliver on their promises? Let’s find out.

## What Makes Ritual Different? (And Is It Just Marketing?)

Ritual was founded in 2016 by Katerina Schneider when she was pregnant and frustrated by the lack of transparency in prenatal vitamins. The company has since expanded to offer multivitamins for various life stages, all built on the same core principles.

Here’s what actually sets them apart from drugstore multivitamins:

### 1. Made Traceable® Supply Chain (Industry First)

Ritual doesn’t just list ingredients—they show you exactly where each ingredient comes from and its final p

results.text:
Ritual Essential Multivitamin Review - The multivitamin I Swear By -

# Ritual Essential Multivitamin Review – The multivitamin I Swear By

### 2/08/2021

### Wellness

### Share the love

##### Sponsored by: Ritual

In my pursuit for healthier living over the last 10+ years, I’ve found that small, consistent lifestyle changes have brought me the biggest wins. For example, when I transitioned to a plant-based diet I didn’t do it overnight. I transitioned in small, manageable stages and focused on building strong habits along the way. One of the small habits that has become an important part of my plant-based lifestyle are daily multivitamins. For me, multivitamins are a great way to help fill gaps for nutrients I’m not getting enough of from food alone. Over the last 3-1/2 months I’ve been taking Ritual Essential multivitamin for Women and I wanted to share my thoughts so far.

Note: Before starting any multivitamin or supplement routine, be sure to talk to consult with your doctor.

#### Ritual Multivitamins Review

##### First Impressions

It was love at first sight when I opened my Ritual package. Like, wow these multivitamins are SO PRETTY. I know that shouldn’t really matter but it does LOL. Ritual really captured my aesthetic! Okay, so let’s talk about things that matter to us all – smell and taste. Ritual multivitamins are infused with a lovely minty taste and scent that I think is brilliant. It makes taking multivitamins so much more pleasant. Like why hasn’t anyone else thought of this? What’s also notable is the smooth casing that makes it easier to swallow.

##### What I Love

- Ritual multivitamins are designed to be gentle on the tummy. While some multivitamins can upset my stomach, I haven’t had any issues with taking Ritual over the last 3+ months.
- In only two multivitamin capsules per day, I get vitamins (minus Vitamin C) that I was previously getting in 6 pills per day. Ritual multivitamins contain 9 traceable nutrients including plant-based Omega 3 DHA, vitamin D3, Iron, Magnesium, B12, Vitamin K2 and more. Ritual intentionally leaves out some nutrients since most adults get adequate amounts of these in their normal diets.*
- Vegan-friendly
- Gluten free
- Formulated to be sugar free with no shady colorants, additives or colorants.
- Bottles are made from 100% recycled materials and shipments are packaged in eco-friendly materials

##### Cost

A monthly subscription for Ritual will run you $30/month and shipping is free (with guaranteed refund within the first 30 days). I did the math and prior to starting my Ritual multivitamins I was paying about $50 per month on vitamins. That’s because I was buying three separate multivitamins to cover what’s already in the Ritual multivitamin (minus vitamin C). While my first bottle of Ritual multivitamins was gifted to me for free, I was so happy with my experience that I signed up for a paid subscription.

##### Subscription – A Pro or Con?

I know many people are not fans of subscripti

# The One Daily Habit We Recommend? Taking This Clean, High-Quality Multivitamin

Words by

##### Jenni Stein

Share

Published on:

Apr 12, 2022

Feb 9, 2023

In partnership with Ritual. Editor’s Note: We only select and work with partners that meet our quality standards, so you can rest assured we only endorse products we believe in.

The world of vitamins can be a tricky one to navigate. There are so many options, and while I am a huge fan of specific vitamins for specific needs, I don’t believe in pulling just any option off a drugstore shelf and calling it a day. Finding a quality multivitamin – one with important key nutrients and one without all the extra stuff (I’m looking at you, sugar) – has historically required hours of research and more typically, cobbling together a bunch of individual vitamins to basically create your own multi. Who has time for that?

Thankfully, I have a few awesome friends who recommended Ritual a few years ago. From the brand’s signature Essential for Women 18+ to the Prenatal multivitamin that I took through two pregnancies, Ritual has totally changed my vitamin game.

Each Ritual capsule is as transparent as its labelCredit: Ritual

#### The Multivitamin, Reinvented

Let’s take a quick look at Ritual’s roots. The company was founded “with the belief that better health begins with better ingredients.” Founder and CEO Katerina Schneider couldn’t find a clean, trustworthy vitamin brand, so she set out to make her own (file under: #goals). She worked with a team of industry-leading scientists to completely reinvent the multivitamin, eventually creating something without any sugary ingredients or artificial colorants. Bonus points for being USP verified (most vitamins are not USP verified). This is what initially intrigued me, as I sought a vitamin I could feel good about putting in my body every single day.

#### 2 Pills a Day, Formulated to Help Fill Nutrient Gaps

Even the healthiest eaters in the world may sometimes struggle to get enough of all the nutrients they need on a daily basis – some nutrients are tough to get from food alone. So, while I aspire to eat as balanced as possible (brief pause to shout out my favorite soft serve and french fries), I know I should still take a quality multivitamin. But, I’m also not super interested in taking anything unnecessary, which leads me to another reason why I love Ritual: the Essential for Women 18+ only contains 9 key nutrients. Leaning into a “ less is more” approach, the brand believes a multivitamin should only help fill gaps in the diet, and uses scientifically-backed research to determine what to include…or not. So what’s in it? Omega-3 DHA and B-12 to support brain health, Vitamin D, Magnesium, Boron and K2 to maintain bone health, chelated Iron and methylated Folate for red blood cell formation, and Vitamin E to provide antioxidant support.*

The Essential for Women’s 9 key nutr

# Rituals Cosmetics: A Head-to-Toe Review from a Devoted Fan
![](https://expertbeacon.com/wp-content/uploads/avatars/2892/1732084630-bpthumb.jpg)ByCharles ScottLast Update onDecember 17, 2024
Hey there! I’m so glad you stopped by my little corner of the web. As a self-care and beauty product junkie, I take testing and reviewing top brands pretty seriously. Today, I can’t wait to share my thoughts on Rituals Cosmetics after using their products in my daily routines for over a year now. From deeply nourishing body washes to the creamiest body lotions that make me glow, I’ve become fully obsessed!
Keep reading for a breakdown of some of my all-time fave items, Rituals 101, tips for shopping the line and more. I hope this review helps you discover some new goodies to upgrade your own rituals!
## An Overview of Rituals’ Brand Story and Top Products
Before we dive into the nitty gritty, let’s start with the basics. Rituals launched in Amsterdam back in 2000 with a unique vision focused on transforming mundane routines into meaningful luxuries that pamper the body and soul.
Over the past 21+ years, they’ve gained a huge cult following (even winning best beauty brand awards!) for their combos of natural ingredients + irresistible scents inspired by ancient Eastern healing traditions. Also key? An ethical commitment to sustainability.
Today, Rituals offers hundreds of items across body care, facial skincare, makeup, home fragrances and more. From lotions and scrubs to cleansers and candles, everything aims to help you decompress and practice self-care amidst the chaos of life.
Some hero ingredients they use across lines are:
* **Rice Milk:**Deeply moisturizing and brightening
* **Bamboo:**Gentle exfoliation
* **White Lotus:**Skin-soothing
* **Yuzu:**Energizing citrus
* **Argan Oil:**Nourishment for skin and hair
And fan-fave scents include sakura (cherry blossom), yuzu and bamboo.
Now let’s get into the products I swear by…
## My 10 Must-Have Rituals Items
***Drumroll please***– these are my holy grail items that I’ve used for ages and will never stop raving over!
### 1. The Ritual of Sakura Body Cream
This thick, velvety body cream leaves my skin seriously drenched in rich moisture. And the scent??*Chef’s kiss.*It’s a gorgeous cherry blossom that lingers allll day long. I smooth it on post-shower morning and night!
**Key ingredients:**Rice milk + cherry tree extract
**My review:**Hydrat

results.title: Rituals Cosmetics: the products I actually love - The Signature
results.text:
Rituals Cosmetics: the products I actually love

Hey there, I’m Jess ✨. On The Signature, I share reflections on beauty, routines, travel, wellness, culture, and everyday life. For those drawn to a calmer, more intentional way of living, I send a free newsletter each month on the 7th. You're welcome to join.

---

A few things leave me with a lasting good impression when I get to someone's house. From the lighting settings (from natural to artificial), to the furniture and textures, to the carpets or floors, to the temperature and to their welcoming smile.

One brand encapsulates all that I love in welcoming houses that just exude quiet luxury: the Dutch brand Rituals Cosmetics, that focuses on home and body wellbeing through delightful scents and mixtures. I first tried the Rituals products in hotels and other people's houses. And I needed them in my house immediately! But where to start?

Intuitta and Gratitude products I love - still have to try The Ritual of Karma After Sun Cooling Gel

#### Products featured in this review

- Gratitude Wardrobe pendant
- Intuitta charms and body scrub
- Luxury Car Air Fresheners
- Precious Amber candles
- The complete Shower Foams collection
- Savage Garden hand wash and hand cream duo
- Green Cardamon candle
- The Ritual of Hammam complete spa collection

Disclaimer: this content is not sponsored nor affiliated - I pay for everything that I use myself. This independence allows me to tell you what I have been loving the most with unbiased honesty.

Rituals Amenities in our Helsinki Hotel

## The Ritual of Hammam Spa Set

A few Christmases ago, my husband gave me a stunning The Ritual of Hammam set. I quickly burned the candle (it was that delightful) and I took my time to use the other products which were:

- Refreshing Foaming Shower Gel - Fresh Eucalyptus & Rosemary
- Ultra Hydrating Black Soap - Refreshing Eucalyptus & Olive Oil
- Purifying Body Mud - Rhassoul Clay & Eucalyptus
- Sea Salt Hot Scrub - Warming Ginger & Eucalyptus
- Soul Uplifting Body Cream - Fresh Eucalyptus & Rosemary

The Rituals of Hammam box set

🛀🏽

Pro tip: at a Rituals store, they recommended that I use the mud first to prepare the skin and the scrub thereafter, to maximise the exfoliating effects on the skin

## The perfect combo for a powder room

When I left Portugal after 4 years living and working there, my parting gift from former colleagues was an amazing white "set" they created, with a beautiful Ivory candle - a now discontinued Green Cardamom one I burned quickly - and a Savage Garden hand wash & hand cream we keep in our visitors bathroom to this day.

Green Cardamom Candle - Savage Garden Hand wash and Hand Balm

Well, I must mean "kept", as we recently finished the Savage Garden Hand Wash and substituted with the Royal Tea Hand Wash, as we wanted to keep the white hues in the bathroom but could not find the Savage Garden one available.

My December 2025 Empties: skincare and wellness products I used to the last dropSo
